event: deceased sister at 2 years of age with severe HI
born by cesarean section at 38 weeks gestation
thick platelike hyperkeratotic scales at birth
admitted to neonatal intensive care unit (NICU)
transport to tertiary center at 2 months old
required multiple transfusions for severe anemia
dorsal aspect of hands and feet involvement
volar aspect of hands and feet involvement
sharply demarcated erythematous scaly plaque on upper face
novel homozygous missense variant in KDSR (c.434A>G)
bone marrow biopsy consistent with congenital megakaryocytic aplasia
bone marrow biopsy consistent with thrombocytopenia
skin biopsy revealed papillary dermis fibrosis
packed red blood cells when hemoglobin <8 g/dL
acitretin 1 mg/kg/day started at 5 months old
evident improvement in scales and erythema
no evidence of dysplasia on skeletal surveys
mild subdural hematoma not requiring evacuation
essential enzyme deficiency in sphingolipid pathway
